title: Hades Has Paperwork
tagline: The Titan has been resting in the Olympus Sea. The Olympus Sea is above the underworld. Hades has paperwork. He is here about the paperwork.
hook_line: Hades arrives at the Olympus gate. He is wearing his formal robes, his skull-shoulder clasps, and his dark-tint glasses, which means this is an official visit. He has a scroll under one arm and Cerberus on a lead. Cerberus's three heads are not in agreement about whether this is a good walk. Hades knocks on the gate once, precisely, and waits.

scenes.title: The Knock
scenes.narration: The knock came at the Olympus gate at mid-morning. One knock. Very precise. Hercules was nearest and opened it. Hades stood at the gate in full formal attire. Dark robes with gold trim. Skull-shoulder clasps polished. Dark-tint glasses. He was holding a scroll in one hand and Cerberus's lead in the other. Cerberus's left head was alert. The middle head was sniffing something in the direction of Olympus kitchen. The right head was asleep. 'I am here,' Hades said, 'on an official matter.' Hercules opened the gate wider. 'Come in.' 'Thank you,' Hades said. 'I have documentation.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Hades at the Olympus gate: formal dark robes, gold Greek key trim, skull-shoulder clasps, dark-tint glasses — one hand holding a scroll, one hand holding Cerberus's lead — Cerberus: three heads in disagreement, left head alert and suspicious, middle head sniffing toward the kitchen, right head fully asleep — the specific visual of a three-headed dog being walked when all three heads want to do different things — Hercules opening the gate — Hades: 'I am here on an official matter. I have documentation.' — Cerberus's lead at full tension in three directions simultaneously — Hades's blue flame hair at formal-reserved height, not flaring, professional — #1A0A3D and gate-stone gray, Cerberus in three-head-disagreement as the main comedy visual

scenes.title: Zeus and Hades
scenes.narration: Zeus met Hades in the main hall. He was wearing his normal everyday crown, which was listing left because he'd been mid-conversation with Poseidon when Hercules reported who was at the gate. He had not had time to adjust it. Hades looked at the listing crown. He adjusted his glasses. He said nothing about the crown because he was here on an official matter and the crown's status was not relevant to the official matter. 'Hades,' Zeus said. 'Zeus,' Hades said. They regarded each other for a moment. 'You look well,' Zeus said. 'I am not here on a social visit,' Hades said. 'I am aware,' Zeus said. 'You never are.' Hades opened his scroll. 'I have concerns.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Zeus meeting Hades in the main hall — Zeus's crown listing left, not corrected because of the surprise visit — Hades looking at the listing crown, adjusting his own glasses in response, saying nothing because it's not relevant to the matter — 'Zeus' / 'Hades' — Zeus: 'you look well' — Hades: 'I am not here on a social visit' — Zeus: 'I am aware. You never are.' — Hades opening his scroll — Cerberus at Hades's feet: left head now very interested in Archie on the windowsill, middle head still thinking about the kitchen, right head still asleep — Archie watching the left-head of Cerberus with his golden eyes — Hades's blue flame hair controlled and professional — #1A0A3D and main hall warmth, the contrast between Zeus's slight chaos and Hades's extreme formality

scenes.title: The Paperwork Concern
scenes.narration: Hades unrolled the scroll. It was long. He read from it with the specific cadence of someone who had written it carefully and was reading it in the order he intended. 'The Titan currently resting in the Olympus Sea has its lower mass — approximately forty percent of its total volume — intersecting with the upper boundary of the underworld's geological jurisdiction.' He paused to let this land. 'This is documented in subsection seven of the original Olympian-Underworld boundary agreement.' He looked up. 'I am not here to remove the Titan. I am here because in subsection twelve, any extended geological occupancy of boundary territory requires a co-signatured residency acknowledgment.' He held out the scroll. 'It is a short form.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Hades reading from the formal scroll — the specific reading cadence of someone who wrote it himself and cares about the order — 'approximately forty percent of its total volume intersecting with the upper boundary of the underworld's geological jurisdiction' — the reference to subsection seven — the key reveal: not here to remove the Titan, here for a 'co-signatured residency acknowledgment' — holding out the scroll — Athena arriving in the hall and immediately recognizing what kind of document this is — Zeus looking at the scroll, then at Athena — Hades switching to his clear glasses now that the formal reading is done and he's moving to the administrative phase — Cerberus: left head has been inching toward Archie the entire time, Archie watching it with complete calm — #1A0A3D and the scroll as the central prop, Hades's formal stance

scenes.title: Athena Reviews the Form
scenes.narration: Athena read the form. It was, as Hades had said, short. She read it twice. 'This requires the Titan's signature,' she said. 'Or a representative signature on behalf of the Titan,' Hades said. 'If the Titan is not available for direct signing.' 'The Titan communicates through dolphins,' Athena said. 'I am aware,' Hades said. His flame flickered slightly. He adjusted his clear glasses. 'That is why I said

Poseidon is the Titan's established communication intermediary.' He looked at Poseidon, who had arrived in the doorway with his log and a sardine-marked note. 'I would like to not have to explain the legal framework for a dolphin to sign a geological residency acknowledgment.' 'No,' Poseidon said. 'That sounds complicated.' 'Extremely,' Hades said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena reading the form carefully — 'this requires the Titan's signature' — Hades: 'or a representative signature, if the Titan is not available for direct signing' — 'the Titan communicates through dolphins' — Hades's flame flickering slightly, the tiniest break in composure — adjusting to his clear glasses — 'that is why I said or a representative. I would like to not have to explain the legal framework for a dolphin to sign a geological residency acknowledgment.' — Poseidon in the doorway: 'that sounds complicated.' / Hades: 'extremely.' — the two of them briefly aligned on this point — Athena now writing notes about the form — the form on the table between Hades and Athena — Hades's clear glasses: administrative mode — #1A0A3D and the document as the center of the scene, Poseidon's sardine note visible in his hand

scenes.title: Cerberus and Archie
scenes.narration: While the paperwork was discussed, Cerberus's left head had been watching Archie for seven minutes. The left head was, as established, the curious one. Archie had been watching the left head for seven minutes with equal focus and no visible concern. The left head eventually got close enough to be at eye level with Archie on the windowsill. They looked at each other. The left head made a small sound — not aggressive, investigative. Archie slow-blinked once. The left head sat down. Hades noticed this from across the room. His flame did something it rarely did: it settled lower, softer — not dim, just quieter. He did not comment. He went back to the paperwork.
scenes.visual_prompt: The parallel B-plot: Cerberus's left head (curious, has been watching Archie since arrival) having reached the windowsill at Archie's level — the two of them face to face — Cerberus left head making a small investigative sound — Archie's slow blink — the left head sitting down — the middle head still thinking about lunch — the right head still asleep — Hades noticing across the room, his blue flame settling lower and softer, the rare quiet flicker — not commenting, returning to paperwork — the flame's momentary softness as the character reveal: Hades has feelings about Cerberus making a friend — Archie and the left head of Cerberus in the windowsill, both looking out — #1A0A3D and the warm window light, the contrast between the formal paperwork scene and this quieter moment

scenes.title: The Form Gets Signed
scenes.narration: Poseidon sent the form via dolphin relay to the Titan. He explained it carefully: the Titan's lower mass was in Hades's jurisdiction and there was a form. The Titan's reply came back: 'YES. I AM SORRY. I DID NOT KNOW ABOUT SUBSECTION TWELVE.' And then: 'IS THE UNDERWORLD SMALL? I HAVE TRIED NOT TO BE TOO HEAVY.' Hades read the Titan's reply. His flame did another thing — not the quiet thing, a different thing. An almost-expression he quickly adjusted. 'Tell the Titan,' he said, adjusting his purple-tint glasses, 'that the underworld is not small. Tell it that the weight is within documented tolerances.' He paused. 'Also tell it that we appreciate the consideration. That is not a legal requirement. That is me saying it.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon sending the form via dolphin — the Titan's reply: 'YES. I AM SORRY. I DID NOT KNOW ABOUT SUBSECTION TWELVE. IS THE UNDERWORLD SMALL? I HAVE TRIED NOT TO BE TOO HEAVY.' — Hades reading the Titan's reply — the almost-expression: something that isn't quite moved but is adjacent to it — the glasses switch to purple-tint: this is the annoyed tint, but the annoyance is at himself for being almost-moved — Hades's response: 'the underworld is not small. The weight is within documented tolerances.' — and then: 'also tell it we appreciate the consideration. That is not a legal requirement. That is me saying it.' — the team's collective warmth at Hades saying something genuine unprompted — Zeus's crown listing left, soft — Poseidon writing it down, sardine on the note — #1A0A3D and the warmth breaking through Hades's formal register

scenes.title: Hades Leaves, Almost
scenes.narration: Hades collected the signed form — counter-signed by Poseidon as Titan representative, witnessed by Athena, noted in Zeus's official register — and rolled it carefully. He put it under his arm. He took Cerberus's lead. The left head looked back at Archie. Archie was still in the window. The left head made a small sound. Hades looked at the left head. He looked at Archie. He said: 'The left head asks if it can come back.' Zeus said: 'Of course.' Hades adjusted his dark-tint glasses — formal again, departure mode. 'It would, of course, require proper documentation.' His flame did the not-quite-quiet thing again. He walked out. At the gate, the left head looked back one more time. Archie was still watching from the window.
scenes.visual_prompt: Hades collecting the signed form — the full procedural close: Poseidon's counter-signature, Athena's witness notation, Zeus's register — Hades rolling the scroll, Cerberus's lead gathered — the left head looking back at Archie — Archie in the window, watching — Hades looking from the left head to Archie — 'the left head asks if it can come back.' — Zeus: 'of course.' — Hades switching back to dark-tint glasses: departure mode — 'it would require proper documentation.' — the flame doing the quiet-soft thing again before he turns — walking out — at the gate, the left head's final look back — Archie watching from the window — the warmth of this exit: Hades maintaining formality while clearly having had a good time — #1A0A3D and the gate light, the left head's final glance the last frame

viral_hook: Hades showed up to Olympus with paperwork because the Titan is 40% in his jurisdiction and there's a form for that. The Titan replied: 'I AM SORRY. I DID NOT KNOW ABOUT SUBSECTION TWELVE. IS THE UNDERWORLD SMALL? I HAVE TRIED NOT TO BE TOO HEAVY.' Hades said: 'Tell it we appreciate the consideration. That is not a legal requirement. That is me saying it.'
highlight_note: The Titan: 'I HAVE TRIED NOT TO BE TOO HEAVY.' Hades, adjusting to purple-tint glasses, audibly moved: 'the weight is within documented tolerances. Also tell it we appreciate the consideration. That is not a legal requirement. That is me saying it.'
short_title: The Titan Has Been Trying Not to Be Too Heavy in Hades's Domain
rewatch_bait: Scene 5 — Cerberus's left head and Archie at the windowsill. Track the left head from scene 1 onward: it has been angling toward Archie the entire episode. The slow-blink is the moment. Hades notices it and his flame goes soft. He doesn't say anything.
youtube_title: Hades showed up with paperwork. The Titan said it tried not to be too heavy. Hades said that's not a legal requirement, he just wanted to say it. | Little Olympus S2 EP12
lesson: Some people express care through documentation and procedure. Hades didn't have to mention that the Titan's weight was within tolerances, or that the consideration was appreciated. He said it anyway. That's him. The official form was the visit. The unofficial part was the real thing.
arc_notes: Hades introduced — character notes: three glasses (dark-tint = formal/departure, clear = administrative, purple-tint = annoyed/moved). Flame tells: flicker = composure slipping, soft-low = genuine feeling, bright flare = not seen yet. Cerberus left-head = curious (bonded with Archie this episode), middle-head = food-focused, right-head = sleepy. The 'left head can come back / it would require proper documentation' is Hades's Season 2 ongoing thread — he will return. Titan-Hades relationship established: Titan has been trying not to be too heavy, Hades is professionally moved by this. Zeus-Hades dynamic: they are brothers who communicate entirely formally and both prefer it that way but clearly like each other.
